feed.title: Language and Semiotic Studies
feed.subtitle: Latest articles from Language and Semiotic Studies (De Gruyter)

entry.title: Blank-signs and their in-field domains in literary texts
entry.summary: Authors: Zhi Yi, Yongzhong Li DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/lass-2025-0022 Pages: Page range: 1-19

entry.title: Digital humor analysis of Bo Burnham’s Netflix comedy specialInside:new sincerity, postirony, and metamodernism at play
entry.summary: Authors: Nashwa Elyamany, Noorhan Abbas DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/lass-2025-0031 Pages: Page range: 20-66

entry.title: Memes as ensemble of illocutionary acts
entry.summary: Authors: Mitul Joseph Koickakudy DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/lass-2025-0003 Pages: Page range: 67-90

entry.title: Cultural conceptualizations of neologisms and coinages in Nigerian multicultural discussion onNairaland Forum
entry.summary: Authors: Rotimi Taiwo, Eunice Omolara Olarewaju DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/lass-2025-0006 Pages: Page range: 91-110

entry.title: Shut up! you are not God: religious identity in commercial tricycle texts in Nigeria
entry.summary: Authors: Eyo Mensah, Kevin Egbo DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/lass-2024-0063 Pages: Page range: 111-132

entry.title: Constructing national identity through symbolic representations in Olympic microblogs: a pathway analysis
entry.summary: Authors: Xiaoyang Wei DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/lass-2025-0060 Pages: Page range: 133-158

entry.title: Emoji and social media paralanguage, by Michele Zappavigna and Lorenzo Logi, Cambridge, Cambridge University Press, 2024, xiv+251 pp., $ 34.99 (paperback), ISBN: 978-1-009-17980-5
entry.summary: Authors: Yuxin Ren DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/lass-2025-0018 Pages: Page range: 159-165
